title: I Know What You Did Next Xmas

jokes.text: That's weird. Normally, I get mail delivered to my ass.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Bender received mail in his stocking, which is unusual
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals his normal mail delivery location is his ass compartment
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender absurdist logic delivered matter-of-factly. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: I wonder what you will have done and who could possibly will have known about it.
jokes.setup: Reading a mysterious note about future knowledge
jokes.punchline: Fry mangles future perfect tense grammar in typical confused fashion
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry grammar confusion, well-integrated to character but not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: Eh, I do a lot of stuff. I'm probably doing something right now.
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual admission he's constantly up to no good
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - casual criminality delivered with zero concern.

jokes.text: Mom's Old Fashioned Blood Remover. Now in oatmeal spice.
jokes.punchline: Blood remover comes in breakfast flavors
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with absurd specificity - blood remover in breakfast flavoring is wonderfully twisted.

jokes.text: Hey, kids, any chance y'all wanna remix this holiday special, and hear the dope story of Kwanzaabot instead?
jokes.punchline: Robot character trying to hijack the narrative with slang
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor with meta-commentary on holiday specials themselves.

jokes.text: Oy...
jokes.punchline: Narrator's exhausted Yiddish reaction to repetitive demands
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat showing narrator's weariness with the routine.

jokes.text: They tricked him out with a pair of carbon-fiber Tims and a chrome naughty-nice sensor.
jokes.punchline: Santa wearing Timberland boots (street slang 'Tims')
jokes.explanation: Urban slang applied to futuristic Santa - mild culture clash humor.

jokes.text: But on Santa's very first delivery, something went tragically meshuggenah.
jokes.punchline: Using Yiddish 'meshuggenah' (crazy) for tragic technical failure
jokes.explanation: Yiddish vocabulary for technical malfunction creates nice linguistic incongruity.

jokes.text: Man, that's some bleeped-up [shit].
jokes.punchline: Self-censoring profanity in what's supposed to be a kids' show
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about broadcast standards within the fictional holiday special.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, kids. I never wanted you to know Santa was real.
jokes.punchline: Reversing the normal parent concern about Santa's existence
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal of the classic Santa revelation trope - parents usually don't want kids to know Santa ISN'T real.

jokes.text: Where is it? - The question is when is it? And the answer is right about... now!
jokes.setup: Someone asking where the time machine is
jokes.punchline: Professor correcting the temporal grammar then having perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on temporal mechanics with good comedic timing on the reveal.

jokes.text: It's so amazing. I don't even care that it's on my foot.
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of being partially crushed by time machine
jokes.explanation: Leela's practical stoicism applied to absurd situation - perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Yeah, but back then, it was some kinda meat market, where butchers would hook up and grind their sausages.
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about butchers' romantic and professional activities
jokes.explanation: Sexual double entendre that gets funnier on rewatch when you catch the innuendo.

jokes.text: But I'll go alone to minimize the chance of anyone becoming their own grandfather. - These things happen. - No, they don't.
jokes.setup: Professor warning about time travel paradoxes
jokes.punchline: Fry casually agreeing since he IS his own grandfather, Leela's correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to series mythology with Fry's casual acknowledgment and Leela's oblivious correction.

jokes.text: Damn it! I backed over Mrs. McGillicuddy's time cat!
jokes.setup: Professor learning to drive the time machine in reverse
jokes.punchline: Running over a 'time cat' like a regular traffic accident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - 'time cat' suggests pets that exist across temporal dimensions. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Ah, here comes the Big Backwards Bang.
jokes.setup: Time machine going back to universe's beginning
jokes.punchline: Reverse Big Bang as casual observation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on cosmic events treated with scientific casualness typical of the Professor.

jokes.text: Ow! I'm being crushed by all the matter that ever was.
jokes.punchline: Mild complaint about being crushed by all existence
jokes.explanation: Magnificent understatement - the most catastrophic event possible treated as minor inconvenience.

jokes.text: It'll be my first happy Xmas since I got frozen and never saw my family again!
jokes.punchline: Casual reminder of his tragic backstory
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic reminder of Fry's origin story delivered with inappropriate cheer.

jokes.text: Oh fuff. Let's stop pretending we do anything around here.
jokes.setup: Being told they have busy work before vacation
jokes.punchline: Admitting they accomplish nothing at their jobs
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the crew's actual productivity level, plus Hermes's mild profanity substitute 'fuff'.

jokes.text: I forgot I had loved ones! It's a human resources miracle!
jokes.punchline: Hermes framing family memory in HR terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment - viewing personal relationships through bureaucratic lens.

jokes.text: I mean, they met him before, but he didn't make much of an impression.
jokes.setup: Amy's family wanting to meet Fry
jokes.punchline: Backhanded admission that Fry is forgettable
jokes.explanation: Mild insult disguised as social nicety - Amy's casual cruelty toward Fry.

jokes.text: So, Bender... - No. - You and I are the only ones with no family. Maybe we could... - No. - Hang out? - Oh. Hang out, you say? Let me think that over for a... No. No. Oh, sorry. Uh, I thought you were still talking.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to make holiday plans with Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's elaborate rejection process with fake consideration
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating rejection structure. Bender's cruelty is perfectly in character and the timing builds beautifully.

jokes.text: Hey, it's me! Your extremely close friend Bender. - How did you get this number? - I didn't! I'm just dialing every number in order.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to be extremely close friend
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's just randomly dialing numbers
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure exposing Bender's desperation through his pathetic methodology.

jokes.text: This nuts bolts!
jokes.punchline: Robot version of 'this sucks' using mechanical terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever robot-specific profanity that works as both wordplay and character-appropriate cursing.

jokes.text: What's not in it?
jokes.setup: Bender asking what's in the nog
jokes.punchline: Implying the nog contains everything possible
jokes.explanation: Efficient response joke suggesting the nog is dangerously all-inclusive.

jokes.text: ♪ Five golden rings! ♪ ♪ Four something something, three something else ♪ ♪ Two no one cares, and a partridge ♪ ♪ Drowned in our nog! ♪
jokes.setup: Singing Twelve Days of Christmas while drinking
jokes.punchline: Forgetting lyrics then ending with partridge drowning in nog
jokes.explanation: Creative parody that starts with lazy lyric forgetting but ends with dark humor about animal death.

jokes.text: None. This is just from the fumes.
jokes.setup: Bender asking how many glasses they've had while clearly drunk
jokes.punchline: They haven't actually drunk any - just inhaling vapors
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - the nog is so potent its fumes alone cause intoxication.

jokes.text: My species dies when we reproduce. So if I had a family, I'd be dead. Does anyone even care about that? - I sure don't.
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate callous dismissal
jokes.explanation: Dark biological humor followed by perfectly callous Bender response. Great character interplay.

jokes.text: I'd like to give him such a snipping.
jokes.setup: Complaining about good Santa ruining Christmas
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's threat using his claw-based anatomy
jokes.explanation: Character-specific threat that plays to Zoidberg's crab anatomy and old-fashioned speech patterns.

jokes.text: But, he has was been here.
jokes.punchline: Mangling complex tense conjugation while drunk
jokes.explanation: Drunk grammar confusion that's perfectly in character for intoxicated Zoidberg.

jokes.text: Wait. Is kidnapping Santa a good idea, or does it just seem like a good idea because we're intoximated? - It's a good idea.
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate confirmation plus Zoidberg's made-up word
jokes.explanation: Good drunk logic joke with Zoidberg's portmanteau 'intoximated' and Bender's unhelpful confirmation.

jokes.text: ♪ Jingle bells, Zoidberg smells just like rotten eggs ♪ I'm immortalized in song.
jokes.setup: Bender singing insulting parody about Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg taking pride in being mentioned at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg character moment - finding silver lining in obvious insult due to his desperate need for attention.

jokes.text: Bite my glittering festive ass!
jokes.setup: Being attacked by Bender and Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Santa using Bender's catchphrase with holiday modification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that puts Bender's signature phrase in Santa's mouth with perfect holiday theming.

jokes.text: Try spreading joy now, you big Xmas ham.
jokes.punchline: Calling Santa a 'ham' (both food and acting term)
jokes.explanation: Decent pun playing on food/performance meanings of 'ham' in holiday context.

jokes.text: We're geniuses! - I'm geniuses!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's grammatically incorrect attempt to claim all credit
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's grammar failure while trying to be selfish creates perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: You know, Zoidberg, you're only half as bad as I thought you were. - That means a lot.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg genuinely grateful for minimal praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Bender's cruel mathematics and Zoidberg's pathetic gratitude.

jokes.text: He's dead. We killed Santa Claus. - Uh... You killed Santy Claus.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg immediately trying to shift blame
jokes.explanation: Immediate cowardly blame-shifting that's perfectly in character for Zoidberg.

jokes.text: This was supposed to be a fun kidnapping! What do we do now? - We gotta get rid of the body. That's usually pretty fun.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg finding silver lining in body disposal
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic optimism from Zoidberg treating murder cleanup as recreational activity.

jokes.text: I admit it! We murd... - Uh, just, uh, dumping some toxic waste, officers. - Huh. Guess you gotta put it somewhere. - Carry on, night dumpers.
jokes.setup: Police catching them with Santa's body
jokes.punchline: Police accepting toxic waste dumping as totally normal
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist police logic - murder suspicious, toxic dumping routine. Great world-building joke.

jokes.text: At $20 a pound!? No, thank you.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether to use flamingo in holiday recipe
jokes.punchline: Treating exotic bird meat as expensive grocery item
jokes.explanation: Mundane price consciousness applied to absurd ingredient choice.

jokes.text: Well, at least I have ideas! What do you suggest? - Hm... We could try an acid bath. - That does sound relaxing, but... - No! We'll dissolve Santa's body!
jokes.punchline: Bender thinking it's for relaxation before realizing it's for body disposal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with Bender's momentary spa-related confusion.

jokes.text: Remind me, is this the ground floor?
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual architectural inquiry during chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to physical comedy disaster, very Bender-esque casualness.

jokes.text: Turdolphin! It's fresh turtle cooked inside a fermented dolphin.
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of turducken with increasingly disturbing marine animals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the turducken concept with marine life. 'Fermented dolphin' is wonderfully disturbing.

jokes.text: Wow. Soon, it's gonna be barf inside a vomit.
jokes.punchline: Predicting his digestive reaction in gross detail
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel structure - food inside food becomes bodily function inside bodily function.

jokes.text: You ruined everything! You're the one who wanted to kidnap Santa! - Oh, yeah? Well, you enabled me, so shut up and keep pushing him into the meat grinder!
jokes.setup: Fighting over blame for Santa's death
jokes.punchline: Argument interrupted by need to continue grinding corpse
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic juxtaposition - relationship therapy during active body disposal.

jokes.text: Wait. Where's my claw?
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg realizing he's ground up part of himself too
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - body disposal goes wrong in most Zoidberg way possible.

jokes.text: Are they hard to catch? - Oh, no. They crave death.
jokes.setup: Asking about hunting turduckens in sewers
jokes.punchline: The animals are suicidally cooperative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark absurdist concept - sewage-dwelling birds that welcome death. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: Hey! Sorry I'm late. I got hungry and ate my skis.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of eating transportation equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - treating edible printed skis as emergency snack. Great economy and absurdist sensibility.

jokes.text: But we danced. And murdered.
jokes.setup: Bender saying they were never friends
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg listing bonding activities in order of innocence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of innocent and horrific bonding experiences. Perfect economy and dark humor.

jokes.text: You want white Christmas or dark Christmas?
jokes.punchline: Meat choice phrased as Christmas color preference
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark humor - holiday imagery applied to cannibalism. Highly quotable and original concept.

jokes.text: Bender, since when do you have three legs? - Uh, uh, th-this one's a spare. In case I break down on the highway.
jokes.setup: Amy noticing Santa's leg attached to Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's car-related excuse for extra body part
jokes.explanation: Good improvisational lie that plays to Bender's mechanical nature. Solid structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Santa seems less dead than we thought.
jokes.setup: Santa arriving despite being murdered and eaten
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about resurrection
jokes.explanation: Magnificent understatement - 'less dead than we thought' for someone who's clearly alive. Perfect Bender delivery.

jokes.text: Oh, I've made a complete Fry of myself.
jokes.punchline: Using Fry's name as substitute for 'fool' while talking to Fry
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay making Fry's name synonymous with foolishness. Original and well-executed.

jokes.text: You didn't take us to last Xmas. You took us to next Xmas! Meaning this Xmas!
jokes.setup: Seeing themselves kidnapping Santa on screen
jokes.punchline: Realizing time machine error created current situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent time travel revelation that recontextualizes the entire episode. Good structure and rewatch value.

jokes.text: Damn it, Bender. I'm a doctor, not a time machine guy!
jokes.punchline: Star Trek 'I'm a doctor not a...' reference applied to Zoidberg
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference joke. Functional but relies on external reference rather than original wit.

jokes.text: The professor set Santa to kill! You saved all 19 of us!
jokes.punchline: Reframing murder as heroic life-saving
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - accidental murder becomes heroic sacrifice in context.

jokes.text: Uh, absolutely not! You're all having a mass hallucination!
jokes.setup: Being called cute for friendship with Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Desperately denying obvious reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - denying friendship despite overwhelming evidence.

jokes.text: You're drinking out of him.
jokes.setup: Question about what happened to Santa
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation they're drinking from Santa's remains
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark humor reveal. Perfect economy - maximum horror in minimum words. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You became friends with Zoidberg! So I'm blackmailing you!
jokes.punchline: Friendship treated as shameful secret worth blackmail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - friendship as blackmail material perfectly fits Bender's character shame.

tmdb_overview: Bender and Zoidberg travel through time to attack Robot Santa.
